Oil will not go to 200 only, in my opinion. It will go much higher.

The Middle East is responsible for 35-40% of the world's crude exports. What happens when the entire region gets choked?

Hydrogen bomb.

#OOTT
quoted_text:
Still seeing people talk about refining being the bottleneck, and relative crude oil oversupply.

Incorrect, and here's why.

Until recently, the Hormuz strait was the only thing blocked. Bypass pipelines could send crude around it (and not products). SPRs of course did their work. And when the strait briefly opened, >100 mm barrels of crude came out.

Those 100 mm barrels will be absorbed in a couple weeks if hormuz remains choked. Much faster if the war goes fully regional.

It is already 99%

text: Iran struck another tanker this one hugging the coast of Oman . #oott $es $xle $vix
quoted_text:
The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) has issued a new advisory after a tanker reported being struck by an unknown projectile on its starboard side near the engine room while transiting near Qalhat, Oman.

According to the advisory, all crew members are safe and accounted for, and no pollution or environmental impact has been reported. The vessel remains under investigation.

This is a separate incident from the attack announced earlier by the United Arab Emirates Ministry of Defe

quoted_text:
BREAKING: President Trump has formally notified US Congress that the US is at war with Iran, per Politico.

Details include:

1. The move gives Trump another 60 days to use the military in the region without congressional approval

2. Trump said strikes that began on July 7th represent "military action consistent with my responsibility to protect Americans and US interests both at home and abroad"

3. Trump also just said that he will "hit Iran hard" tonight amid mounting tensions

The Iran War
